title: Set Board Template (Column Structure, Permissions, and Automations Applied Across Linked Boards)
excerpt: Multiple boards that require the same setup are difficult to manage as columns are added/updated, permissions change, automations set up. Although there is a “save as a template” feature, each board still has to be updat…

posts.author: SteveC (Steve Chu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Multiple boards that require the same setup are difficult to manage as columns are added/updated, permissions change, automations set up. Although there is a “save as a template” feature, each board still has to be updated manually if a change is made to the template or an existing board.

Requested feature is to have the ability to set a Board as MASTER board. Other boards can be linked to the MASTER board so any structure, permission, or automation changes are applied across all linked boards.

posts.author: avirampm (Aviram Marom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @SteveC,

Aviram here, a product manager at monday.com - thanks for the input
You are not the first one to ask for this kind master/live templates that enforce changes across all boards that use those templates.
We intend to hopefully release this feature by the end of the year, so stay tuned!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is in the works at monday.com for ENTERPRISE accounts.

Some good news to share: there is an alternative app called “Enhanced Boards Templates” that can be installed from monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done (marketplace submission is ongoing).

On your template board you install a recipe just to confirm this is a template. You need to leave that recipe in the template.

Now, every board created from this template (by whatever method) will see this recipe and the new board will subscribe itself to the other recipe. The recipe can be deleted from the newly created board if you wish, it does not do anything and is not needed anymore.

The second recipe is to be used in your template (it does do nothing in boards created from that template). You can create any column type with it and that column will be added to the template and all boards created from it.

Have fun with it

posts.author: Barton (Eugene Somov)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, currently we run Monday based PM system.

There are 50+ standard formatted linked boards based on templates and 12 dashbords. Most of boards based on one common template. Just imagine, you have a task to change template and all of boards based on this template in hours.

Now it can be done manually only. No anower way. Its horrible, and take a lot of time each times you need to do so.

For complicated cases, like mine, automatical transition of changes from template to template-based boards is strongly required feature. Hope Monday team can implement it soon.

posts.author: Johnsa9 (Ashley Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml:
MASS UPDATE BOARDS FROM TEMPLATES:

We use [JOB FOLDERS] with 4 [TEMPLATES] in each folder as so:

image337×664 27.5 KB

We are constantly changing automation’s, layouts, etc. to continue to advance our boards and business. These job folders are our MAIN access point for all jobs.

This makes it confusing for our guys in the field because some boards are different than others and work in different ways and most importantly I am needing to go into every single job folder to apply these changes. Currently we have well over 50 job folders.

Solution: Two check-boxes in the template

  • Update board changes to all (boards created from this template)
    (column moves, permissions, name changes, status options, etc.)

  • Update automation changed to all (boards created from this template)

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )

Thank you for your thoughtful feedback. After reading through your requests and ideas with the product team, I am excited to share that this feature is on our roadmap and is estimated to be released in 2024.

Thank you for being part of our development process. Your ideas and thoughts help us better understand the features that have the most impact for our customers all over the world.

Keep sharing your ideas with us and learn more about our feature request process here.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, Everyone; after reading your feedback and researching your needs, we are getting ready to release a new unique feature to to enable standardization across workflows at scale. The details:

  • Our team is working on creating a “special template” that will remain connected to any board created from it.
  • Changes to the template will also impact previous boards that were built from the template.
  • The team is working on adding more features to be carried over from the template to the boards created from it.

The beta version of this special template has already started gradually being released to Enterprise accounts. If you are an enterprise account interested in accessing the beta version, please reach out to our monday representative.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I believe it is. Do you have access to it?! What do you think?

posts.author: User1234 (Simon L.)
posts.cookedHtml: H @Julietteb. Thanks for the update. Any plans on releasing this to ‘Pro plan subscribers’?

posts.author: b.schwarzwald (Barbara Schwarzwald [Decadis AG])
posts.cookedHtml:
For those that do not have Enterprise Accounts, our Marketplace App Batch Hero for monday can at least partially help with your issues:

It let’s you add or delete columns to/from multiple boards at once as well as copy a column and its configuration from one board (e.g. a template) to multiple others at once.
It also gives a nice overview over all boards, there owners, locations, columns etc.

Due to API-restrictions, we can’t offer bulk update column configuration or permissions or automations yet, but we are continually improving the app to make board configuration & management smoother for you.

Best regards
Barbara

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team I’m excited to share that our Managed Template feature is now fully released for ENT plans!

This feature allows you to create a template that stays connected to the boards built from it, so when structural changes are made (like adding columns, groups, or items), you can publish updates that apply automatically across all connected boards. This is a big step toward easier, more scalable workflow standardization!

Please note: Some elements (like automations, integrations, and certain column settings) aren’t yet supported, but we’re actively working on expanding this functionality and will keep you updated.

Want to see how it works? Check out this quick tutorial video.

Thank you, as always, for your feedback, we couldn’t make these changes and updates without our community!

posts.author: ascheubel (SCHEUBEL Aurélie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Amy, thanks a lot !
I tried tu use it, but saw a problem : I added a new status “test” in the template. Then, it appears on a new board based on my template. Right.

  • Then, when i tried to delete this status from the child board, i couldn’t do it. Right.
  • BUT, when i tried to delete it from the template (parent board), i couldn’t also do it. A message appears saying that the status is already used in chlid board and then, i cannot delete it… (however, no more tasks have this status in the child board…)

Then, I have now a status “test” and have no way to delete it…in the child, in the template, and then, in the new futures boards created from the template…

Thanks
